stimulus: The pascal is the standard international unit used to measure pressure exerted by a fluid. Its definition depends on how much force is distributed across how large an ______blank one pascal is equivalent to the pressure produced when one newton of force acts uniformly over one square meter of surface area.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The two clauses ("Its definition...area" and "for instance...surface area") are independent. A semicolon joins them properly; the conjunctive adverb "for instance" is then set off by a following comma.

Choice B is incorrect because a comma alone before "for instance" produces a comma splice between independent clauses. Choice C is incorrect because a comma alone between two independent clauses produces a comma splice. Choice D is incorrect because the unpunctuated junction produces a run-on.
